an1.51:1.2: And yet it is defiled by adventitious defilements.
an1.51:1.3: The uninstructed worldling does not know it as it truly is.
an1.51:1.4: Therefore I say: 'For the uninstructed worldling, there is no development [*bhāvanā*] of mind [*citta*].'
an1.52:1.2: And it is freed from adventitious defilements.
an1.52:1.3: The instructed noble disciple knows it as it truly is.
an1.52:1.4: Therefore I say: 'For the instructed noble disciple, there is development [*bhāvanā*] of mind [*citta*].'
an1.53:1.1: "If a *bhikkhu*, *bhikkhus*, cultivates a mind [*citta*] of goodwill [*mettā*] even for just a finger-snap,
an1.53:1.3: 'a *bhikkhu* who dwells not devoid of *jhāna*, who follows the Teacher's instruction, who acts on advice, and who does not eat the country's alms-food in vain.'
an1.53:1.4: What then of those who practice it extensively?
an1.54:1.1: "If a *bhikkhu*, *bhikkhus*, develops a mind [*citta*] of goodwill [*mettā*] even for just a finger-snap,
an1.54:1.3: 'a *bhikkhu* who dwells not devoid of *jhāna*, who follows the Teacher's instruction, who acts on advice, and who does not eat the country's alms-food in vain.'
an1.54:1.4: What then of those who practice it extensively?
an1.55:1.1: "If a *bhikkhu*, *bhikkhus*, attends to a mind [*citta*] of goodwill [*mettā*] even for just a finger-snap,
an1.55:1.3: 'a *bhikkhu* who dwells not devoid of *jhāna*, who follows the Teacher's instruction, who acts on advice, and who does not eat the country's alms-food in vain.'
an1.55:1.4: What then of those who practice it extensively?
an1.56:1.1: "Whatever *dhammas*, *bhikkhus*, are unwholesome, partaking of the unwholesome, on the side of the unwholesome — all of them are preceded by mind.
an1.56:1.2: Mind arises first among those *dhammas*; unwholesome *dhammas* follow right after."
an1.57:1.1: "Whatever *dhammas*, *bhikkhus*, are wholesome, partaking of the wholesome, on the side of the wholesome — all of them are preceded by mind.
an1.57:1.2: Mind arises first among those *dhammas*; wholesome *dhammas* follow right after."
an1.58:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single *dhamma* that so causes unarisen unwholesome *dhammas* to arise and arisen wholesome *dhammas* to decline as, *bhikkhus*, heedlessness.
an1.58:1.2: For the heedless person, *bhikkhus*, unarisen unwholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen wholesome *dhammas* decline."
an1.59:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single *dhamma* that so causes unarisen wholesome *dhammas* to arise and arisen unwholesome *dhammas* to decline as, *bhikkhus*, heedfulness.
an1.59:1.2: For the heedful person, *bhikkhus*, unarisen wholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen unwholesome *dhammas* decline."
an1.60:1.1: "I do not see, *bhikkhus*, any other single *dhamma* that so causes unarisen unwholesome *dhammas* to arise and arisen wholesome *dhammas* to decline as, *bhikkhus*, laziness.
an1.60:1.2: For the lazy person, *bhikkhus*, unarisen unwholesome *dhammas* arise and arisen wholesome *dhammas* decline."
